points.title: Leopard Capital of the World
points.description: Highest density of Sri Lankan leopards globally with 25 individuals in Block I alone, offering exceptional big cat viewing opportunities.

points.title: Asian Elephant Gatherings
points.description: Seasonal migrations of 300-350 elephants create spectacular wildlife viewing during dry season water source concentrations.

points.title: Situlpahuwa Ancient Temple
points.description: 2,000-year-old Buddhist monastery complex that once housed 12,000 arahants, combining wildlife viewing with cultural heritage.

points.title: Block I Safari Zone
points.description: Most popular safari area with highest wildlife density, excellent leopard sightings, and comprehensive viewing infrastructure.

points.title: Block V Wilderness Experience
points.description: Quieter, more intimate safari zone offering excellent wildlife diversity with fewer crowds for serious wildlife enthusiasts.

points.title: Endemic Birdlife Sanctuaries
points.description: Seven endemic species among 215 bird species in diverse habitats from coastal lagoons to inland forests.

points.title: Magul Vihara Temple
points.description: Ancient Buddhist temple built in 87 BC showcasing sophisticated architecture and hydraulic engineering of the Ruhuna Kingdom.

points.title: Coastal Marine Ecosystems
points.description: Pristine beaches, lagoons, and mangrove systems supporting sea turtle nesting and diverse marine life along Indian Ocean coastline.
